title: THE BATMAN
logline: In his second year as Batman, Bruce Wayne hunts a serial killer targeting Gotham's elite while uncovering corruption that connects to his own family's legacy.
antagonistOrConflict: The Riddler and systemic corruption in Gotham
tone: Dark, gritty, atmospheric, procedural with psychological intensity

actBreaks.description: Batman visits the Joker in Arkham to decode the Riddler's cipher, marking his descent into the investigation's psychological depths and establishing surveillance on Selina.

validation.ruling: Scene #29 (p33, Arkham consultation) marks Act 1's end: Batman consults an unseen prisoner (likely Joker) about the Riddler case, establishing a major shift from solo investigation to seeking outside perspective, fundamentally changing his approach and acknowledging he cannot solve this alone—a crucial turning point that propels the story into Act 2's deeper investigation.

actBreaks.description: Batman escapes GCPD custody via wingsuit after the explosion at City Hall, becoming a fugitive and fundamentally shifting his relationship with law enforcement and the city.

validation.ruling: Scene #64 (p93, false signal) ends Act 2: Batman and Gordon arrive separately at the construction site, each thinking the other sent the Bat-Signal, only to realize they've been lured into a trap—this revelation marks the transition into Act 3 where they discover Selina holding Kenzie and the final confrontation with Riddler's endgame begins.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a detective noir structured as an escalating investigation where each murder reveals deeper layers of institutional rot. The script operates like a procedural thriller—Batman and Gordon move from crime scene to crime scene, clue to clue—but it's anchored by Bruce's internal journey from vengeance to hope. The three-act structure is clean but unconventional: Act One establishes the pattern (Mayor's murder, Commissioner's murder, surveillance operation); Act Two explodes it (D.A.'s public execution, Batman becomes hunted, family secrets exposed); Act Three converges all threads (Riddler's endgame at the Garden, the flood, Batman's transformation from dark knight to beacon). The Riddler functions less as a traditional antagonist and more as a dark mirror—his escalating riddles force Batman to confront what his crusade has actually accomplished and what Gotham truly needs.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The first act moves methodically through crime scenes and clue-gathering (scenes 1-29), establishing rhythm through procedural repetition. Act Two accelerates dramatically—the City Hall explosion (scene 38) detonates the pacing, followed by the Batmobile chase (scenes 47-49) and the orphanage revelation (scene 51). The script then downshifts for emotional processing (scenes 55-63: Alfred's injury, family history) before the final sprint. Act Three maintains relentless momentum from the diner confrontation (scene 79) through the Garden climax (scenes 83-84) to the flood rescue (scenes 85-88). The structure uses location repetition strategically—we return to Wayne Tower, the unfinished skyscraper, Arkham—creating spatial anchors that mark Bruce's evolution.

incitingIncident.description: Batman arrives at Mayor Mitchell's townhouse led by Lieutenant Gordon, confronts a skeptical officer, and examines the mayor's body with head mummified in duct tape reading 'NO MORE LIES' while detectives discuss evidence and a greeting card addressed to Batman.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. MAYOR'S TOWNH OUSE - MOVING DOWN THE HALL - NIGHT

validation.ruling: Scene #1 is merely exposition showing the mayor watching debate coverage—it doesn't shatter the status quo or force action. Scene #11 (p9, Mayor's murder) is the true inciting incident: the Riddler's brutal killing of Mayor Mitchell irreversibly shatters Gotham's world, establishes the central conflict, introduces the serial killer antagonist, and forces Batman into action to solve the case.

midpoint.description: Batman and Gordon kick in the doors of the abandoned Gotham Orphanage, follow white arrows up a gothic staircase through empty rooms, hear distant choir music and giggling, then see a silhouetted figure emerge from a door.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. MAIN ENTRYWAY - GOTHAM ORPHANAGE RUINS - NIGHT

validation.ruling: Scene #51 shows Batman and Gordon discovering dropheads in the orphanage—a clue discovery, not a transformational shift. Scene #52 (p78, Alfred's bombing) is the true midpoint: Batman receives the devastating call that Alfred has been critically injured by Riddler's bomb at Wayne Tower, making the investigation intensely personal, raising stakes dramatically, and marking the point of no return where Batman can no longer remain emotionally detached from the case.

climax.description: Hooded gunmen position themselves in the Garden rafters with rifles and ammunition while Gordon and Bella Real argue with a Fire Marshall about closing the doors as water breaches; Batman arrives and warns them of the attack, then the lights go out, gunfire erupts, and Batman fights the shooters in darkness while Selina arrives and joins the fight, ultimately saving a young boy as explosions destroy the seawall and water floods in.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. GOTHAM SQUARE GARDEN - HIGH ABOVE - SAME

validation.ruling: Scene #84 (p122, Gotham Square Garden siege) is the climax: hooded gunmen position themselves in the rafters with rifles targeting the crowd below while Gordon tries to evacuate Bella Real and civilians as the seawall breaches—this is the peak confrontation where all narrative threads converge, maximum stakes are realized, and Batman must face the ultimate test of stopping Riddler's followers from massacring innocent people.

scenes.heading: INT. OPULENT TOWNHOUSE - DIMLY-LIGHTED STUDY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mayor Mitchell watches a televised debate in his study while drinking scotch, seeing himself challenged by Bella Real over the Gotham Renewal Program.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP - ON THE RUSTED SEARCHLIGHT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Raindrops illuminate and dance on the hot lens of a rusted searchlight as its light intensifies.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAINY GOTHAM STREETS - HIGH ANGLE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A voice-over narrator describes watching Gotham's crowded Halloween streets from a hidden vantage point, following a male figure through costumed revelers.

scenes.heading: EXT. BODEGA - MOVING POV TOWARD THE WINDOW - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The narrator watches through a bodega window as a man in a sad emoji mask pulls a gun on the cashier.

scenes.heading: EXT. COLOSSAL GOTHAM BANK - MOVING POV - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Three vandals in Guy Fawkes masks spray-paint a bank's pillars and one hurls a Molotov cocktail through the glass door.

scenes.heading: INT. A SPEEDING ELEVATED TRAIN - LURKING POV - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Eight gang members with skull face paint watch a cellphone video of themselves assaulting a jogger, then their leader signals a younger member to target a lone passenger as the train slows.

scenes.heading: EXT. A SHADOWY SPACE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Fingers smear black camo paint around eyes in darkness, and a duffle bag reveals a glimpse of the Bat cowl inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP - ON THE RUSTED SEARCHLIGHT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Bat-Signal blazes from atop an abandoned skyscraper as a silhouetted man waits beside it; the sad emoji robber and pedestrians spot the signal in alarm.

scenes.heading: EXT. COLOSSAL GOTHAM BANK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A vandal sees a police chopper pass the Bat-Signal, drops his spray can in alarm, and flees with the others as a fire rages behind them and a metallic sound bursts forth.

scenes.heading: EXT. ELEVATED TRAIN PLATFORM - POURING RAIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The lone passenger exits onto a deserted platform and is chased by eight skull-faced gang members who pounce on him while the young member hesitates and spots the Bat-Signal.

scenes.heading: INT. MAYOR'S TOWNH OUSE - MOVING DOWN THE HALL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman arrives at Mayor Mitchell's townhouse led by Lieutenant Gordon, confronts a skeptical officer, and examines the mayor's body with head mummified in duct tape reading 'NO MORE LIES' while detectives discuss evidence and a greeting card addressed to Batman.
scenes.turningPointNote: First murder victim revealed; Riddler directly addresses Batman, establishing their connection and the serial killer investigation

scenes.heading: EXT. MAYOR'S MANSION - FRONT STEPS - PRE-DAWN
scenes.summary: Commissioner Savage holds a press conference on the mansion steps while a distant figure on a motorcycle watches from across the street and pulls on a helmet.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM - MOVING THROUGH THE STREETS - PRE-DAWN
scenes.summary: The motorcycle chases through lower Gotham past tent cities and halted construction sites as the press conference plays on jumbotrons.

scenes.heading: INT. NARROW, OLD TUNNEL - PRE-DAWN
scenes.summary: Bruce Wayne rides through a secret tunnel into Wayne Tower's underground foundation where bats stir, removes his helmet and contact lenses with sensor bands, and reviews grainy footage in his handwritten journal.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE TOWER - GRAND FOYER - MOVING - MORNING
scenes.summary: Bruce moves through Wayne Tower's neglected grand foyer to the dining room where Alfred works on the cipher and offers him berries, sparking a brief exchange about the puzzle.

scenes.heading: INT. GOTHAM CITY POLICE DEPARTMENT - COMMAND CENTER - DAY
scenes.summary: Gordon receives a call from Bruce at GCPD command center where detectives and FBI codebreakers work on the cipher, and Bruce asks if Mitchell had a car.

scenes.heading: INT. MAYOR'S MANSION - FREIGHT ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman and Gordon descend to the mayor's subterranean garage, find a flat tire on an Aston DB11, discover bloody poultry shears inside it, and Batman finds a thumb drive taped under the dashboard.
scenes.turningPointNote: Discovery of crucial evidence (thumb drive) that advances the investigation and reveals the mayor's secrets

scenes.heading: EXT. ICEBERG LOUNGE NIGHTCLUB - MOVING SHOT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman arrives at the Iceberg Lounge and confronts two bouncers who refuse him entry and threaten to call the police before slamming the door.

scenes.heading: INT. DIM CLUB DRESSING ROOM/BACKROOM OFFICE ARE A - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman shows Penguin a photo of a twenty-two year old woman with the mayor; Penguin claims not to know her, and a stunning woman in an evening gown enters, hesitates upon seeing Batman, then brings Penguin a drink tray.

scenes.heading: EXT. ICEBERG LOUNGE NIGHTCLUB - POURING RAIN NOW - LATER
scenes.summary: The woman from the club emerges anxiously, flags down a gypsy cab, and gets in as a helmeted motorcyclist watches her depart.

scenes.heading: INT. NAVY BLUE GYPSY CAB - BACKSEAT - LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: The woman speaks urgently on her cell phone in the cab's backseat, promising to leave tonight if necessary, while the helmeted drifter follows behind.

scenes.heading: EXT. RUN-DOWN, EAST END APARTMENT - BINOCULAR POV - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Through binoculars, the drifter watches the woman enter her apartment where cats swarm her and she finds the bruised twenty-two year old from the photo growing hysterical while watching TV.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST END APARTMENT - GARAGE FRONT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: A helmeted Catwoman on a black sport bike roars past the drifter getting on his bike.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS - MOVING SUPER FAST - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The drifter races his bike at over 100 mph through traffic, weaving and slaloming cars to keep Catwoman in sight ahead.

scenes.heading: INT. MAYOR'S MANSION - LOOKING UP AT SKYLIGHT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Catwoman lowers gracefully on a rope through the skylight into the mayor's mansion.

scenes.heading: INT. MAYOR'S MANSION - STUDY - THE CRIME SCENE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Catwoman opens the wall safe in the mayor's study when Batman confronts her; she grabs something and they fight brutally across the room until he pins her down and she kicks him off, then flees through the skylight.

scenes.heading: EXT. WET GOTHAM STEETS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman and Catwoman race past stunned pedestrians on their motorcycles at over 100 mph.

scenes.heading: INT. SELINA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman and Catwoman enter Selina's ransacked apartment where she searches for Annika; Batman examines unpaid bills revealing Selina's name and watches TV news announcing Commissioner Savage's murder and showing the Riddler's video claiming credit and threatening the biggest piece of the puzzle on Halloween.
scenes.turningPointNote: Second murder revealed (Commissioner Savage); Riddler's public threat escalates stakes and timeline to Halloween

scenes.heading: INT. VISITING CELL - ARKHAM PRISON F OR THE INSANE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman visits an unseen prisoner at Arkham who analyzes the Riddler case file, suggests the killer wants Batman to unmask corruption, and warns that the Riddler is speaking directly to Batman.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAREHOUSE ROOF - ACROSS F RO M ICEBERG LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman sets up portable surveillance equipment on a warehouse roof while Selina questions whether he cares what happens to her; he tells her to look at him and they stare intensely into each other's eyes.

scenes.heading: INT. ICEBERG LOUNGE - CLOSE ON SELINA - MOVING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Selina moves through the Iceberg Lounge wearing contact lens cameras while Batman watches on his laptop; Penguin reluctantly lets her work downstairs in the exclusive VIP area where she serves Carmine Falcone, D.A. Gil Colson, and Detective Kenzie, overhearing them discuss a rat and drops.
scenes.turningPointNote: Selina infiltrates the VIP room and overhears crucial information about the rat, Maroni case, and police corruption involving Falcone

scenes.heading: EXT. WAREHOUSE ROOF - BATMAN'S LA PTOP - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Batman watches on his laptop as Selina enters a bathroom, confronts her about knowing Carmine Falcone, and she removes her contact lenses, cutting off his feed.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT OF ICEBERG LOUNGE - UNKNOWN POV - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Through fogged SUV windows, someone in an olive green hood watches and breathes as Selina exits the club and Gil Colson stumbles out offering her a ride, which she declines before getting into a gypsy cab.

scenes.heading: EXT. ABANDONED SKYSCRAPER CONSTRUCTION SITE - PRE-DAWN
scenes.summary: Gordon meets Batman at the abandoned skyscraper by the Bat-Signal where Batman reveals the rat is a confidential informant in the Maroni case and suggests finding the rat will lead to the Riddler.

scenes.heading: INT. "THE CAVE" - UNDERNEATH WAYNE TOWER - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Bruce examines surveillance footage of Selina in his underground workspace when Alfred arrives and they discuss the cipher and Bruce's father's cufflink found at the crime scene.

scenes.heading: INT. BRUCE'S VINTAGE SP ORTS CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: Bruce drives to City Hall and sees Riddler-inspired protestors with question mark signs in the crowd, then a traffic cop recognizes him and stares.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT OF GOTHAM CITY HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Bruce exits his car as paparazzi recognize and photograph him, then he spots Carmine Falcone arriving with bodyguards and a veiled woman in a high-slit dress.

scenes.heading: INT. GOTHAM CITY HALL - MOVING WITH BRUCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Bruce scans the memorial crowd at City Hall, observing security and mourners, when a bitter man in a hooded jacket tells him the mayor got what he deserved and gives Bruce a chilling look before Bruce is interrupted by Bella Real offering condolences.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM CITY HALL - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Police and SWAT surround City Hall where Gil Colson sits with a bomb collar and ringing phone; Batman emerges from shadows and approaches him while crisis command watches via robot camera, then the bomb's timer activates and Batman realizes answering three riddles is the only way to disarm it.
scenes.turningPointNote: Third victim (Gil Colson) in immediate mortal danger; Riddler forces Batman into a public deadly game with riddles and time limit

scenes.heading: INT. GCP D - INTERROGATION ROOM - BATMAN'S POV - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman regains consciousness in GCPD interrogation room as cops debate unmasking him and Chief Bock accuses him of Colson's death; Batman suggests Colson feared the police, then Gordon stages a fight allowing Batman to escape through a window.

scenes.heading: EXT. UNFINISHED SKYSCRAPER - HIGH OVER THE CITY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon meets Batman on the unfinished skyscraper with a bruised face and reports an APB is out; they discuss Kenzie's connection to Falcone and theorize Penguin might be the rat who informed on Maroni.

scenes.heading: EXT. ICEBERG LOUNGE - ALLEY - POV OUT WIND SHIELD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Someone with menacing breathing watches through rain and fog as Penguin, Kenzie, and the Twins exit the Iceberg Lounge with duffles and get into vehicles.

scenes.heading: INT. GORDON'S C RUISER - SAME MOMENT
scenes.summary: Gordon waits in his cruiser and radios updates as Penguin and Kenzie's cars drive past him.

scenes.heading: EXT. WATERFRONT STREET - DESERTED WAREHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Penguin and Kenzie arrive at Gotham Recycling warehouse, honk, and approach as a roll-door lifts.

scenes.heading: INT. GORDON'S C RUISER - BELOW AN UNDERPASS - SAME MOMENT
scenes.summary: Gordon radios Batman their location; Batman confirms he's on the warehouse roof watching through a skylight as Penguin inspects workers filling vials with chemicals and meets with a man in a suit.

scenes.heading: INT. GORDON'S CRUISER - SAME MOMENT
scenes.summary: Selina attacks the Twins with a chain, knocking them out, then opens the trunk to find duffles stuffed with cash when Batman appears behind her; she reveals Annika is missing and accuses Kenzie and Falcone of being involved.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. DRIVING ON GOTHAM STREETS - IMMEDIATELY
scenes.summary: The Batmobile violently pursues Penguin's Escalade through Gotham streets, ramming it repeatedly while Penguin fires his Uzi through the window.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERTED GOTHAM TRAIN YARD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman and Gordon interrogate bound Penguin in a train yard about being the rat who informed on Maroni; Penguin denies it and reveals the real rat is Falcone, explaining the entire police force and city officials protect him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Major revelation that Falcone is the protected rat, exposing the full scope of Gotham's systemic corruption

scenes.heading: EXT. GORDON' S CAR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Batman and Gordon access the website rataalada.com on a laptop where a blinking cursor appears and types messages; they exchange texts with someone asking if they found 'el rata alada' and receiving cryptic responses about missing the big picture and a new riddle.

scenes.heading: INT. BATMOBILE - MOVING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. MAIN ENTRYWAY - GOTHAM ORPHANAGE RUINS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman and Gordon kick in the doors of the abandoned Gotham Orphanage, follow white arrows up a gothic staircase through empty rooms, hear distant choir music and giggling, then see a silhouetted figure emerge from a door.

scenes.heading: INT. BATMOBILE - IMMEDIATELY
scenes.summary: Batman calls Dory demanding to speak to Alfred, but she tells him something terrible has already happened; he looks up to see Wayne Tower with black smoke and flames being doused by firemen.
scenes.turningPointNote: Alfred is caught in an explosion — a direct attack on Bruce's personal life that escalates the Riddler's threat beyond Gotham's corruption to Bruce himself.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYN E TOWER - GRAND FOYER - NIG HT
scenes.summary: Bruce stands stunned in Wayne Tower's foyer as a detective shows him a scorched greeting card addressed to Batman reading 'SEE YOU IN HELL' and asks if he'd be a target.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSP ITAL - WINDOW OUTSIDE AN I .C.U. ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bruce gazes through a hospital window at bandaged, unconscious Alfred on oxygen as a doctor tells him to go home and asks if there's any next of kin to notify.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE TOWER - DINING ROOM - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Bruce, shirtless and tortured, spray-paints a massive evidence board across the dining room floor connecting photos and details, then freezes on an area labeled 'THE S'.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE TOWER - THE STUDY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Bruce breaks crime scene tape in the study, searches through enormous stacks of Renewal files in desk drawers, and thousands of pages scatter as stacks topple over.

scenes.heading: INT. BATCAVE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Bruce sets Renewal files on his workbench in the Batcave, notices a live contact lens feed showing Selina holding a handwritten sign 'WHERE R U?', and she urgently asks where they can meet.

scenes.heading: EXT. UNFINISHED SKYSCRAPER - HIGH OVER THE CITY - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Batman meets Selina atop an unfinished skyscraper where she asks for his help finding those who hurt Annika; they argue about choices and consequences, with Selina accusing him of growing up rich, before she reveals Annika was her girlfriend and storms off.

scenes.heading: INT. "THE CAVE" - UNDERNEATH WAYNE TOWER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bruce pulls off his cowl and watches a viral Riddler video on TV that freezes a Thomas Wayne campaign ad and accuses the Waynes and Arkhams of lying about their legacy while Gotham suffered.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Riddler publicly attacks Bruce's family legacy, forcing Bruce to confront the possibility that his father's history is darker than he believed.

scenes.heading: INT. FALCONE'S SHORELINE LOFT - PRIVATE ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bruce confronts Falcone in his loft and demands the truth; Falcone admits he had a reporter killed for Thomas Wayne and implies Thomas had Salvatore Maroni arrested to protect his mayoral campaign.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bruce learns his father may have been complicit in murder and corruption, shattering his foundational belief in Thomas Wayne's goodness.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE TOWER - GRAND FOYER - DAWNING LIGHT
scenes.summary: Bruce arrives at Wayne Tower in a daze and stops to stare at the chained and padlocked doors to his parents' bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. BRUCE'S PARENTS' DARKENED BEDROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Bruce enters his parents' untouched, dust-covered bedroom and sees his father's jacket, glasses on a newspaper, his mother's stained teacup, and his own childhood crayon drawings of a smiling stick-figure family on the mirror.

scenes.heading: INT. ALFRED 'S HOSPITAL ROOM - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Bruce tells hospitalized Alfred that Falcone revealed he had Thomas Wayne's father killed by Maroni; Alfred insists Thomas was a good man who made a mistake asking Falcone for help, then begged him to call it off, but Falcone used it to gain power over the city.
scenes.turningPointNote: Alfred reveals the full truth about Thomas Wayne — he was flawed but good, and tried to stop Falcone — giving Bruce a more complex understanding of his father and his own mission.

scenes.heading: EXT. ABANDONED SKYSCRAPER CONSTRUCTION SITE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman and Gordon arrive separately at an abandoned construction site, both confused because each thought the other sent the Bat-Signal, then look up to see the spotlight in the sky.

scenes.heading: INT. RISING CONSTRUCTION ELEVATOR - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Batman and Gordon find Selina holding Kenzie at gunpoint, chained to a guardrail; she plays Annika's voicemail revealing Falcone killed her, then nearly shoots Kenzie before Batman and Gordon wrestle the gun away and she flees on her motorcycle.
scenes.turningPointNote: The voicemail reveals Falcone murdered Annika and is the real informant, shifting the investigation's target and sending Selina on a revenge mission.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS - AT HIGH SPEED - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Selina speeds on her motorcycle through columns under an elevated highway and disappears behind the Iceberg Lounge building.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERTED ALLEY BEHIND ICEBERG LOUNGE - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Selina removes her helmet, checks her Glock's ammunition, unzips her bodysuit to reveal a slit dress underneath, and enters the club as Batman watches hidden in shadow from the Batmobile.

scenes.heading: INT. ICEBERG LOUNGE NIGHTCLUB - MOVING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Selina asks a guard to tell Falcone she wants to see him about Annika; the guard speaks into his mic, then opens the private elevator door for her.

scenes.heading: INT. FALCONE'S SHORELINE LOFT - ELEVATOR HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Selina steps out of the elevator past intimidating guards to meet Falcone in the drawing room and asks to speak with him alone, while outside the club the Twins investigate a loud knocking and get locked out as the door shuts behind them.

scenes.heading: INT. HEART OF THE CLUB - MOVING - IMMEDIATELY
scenes.summary: Batman, disguised as a drifter, enters the club and heads toward a restricted door as a security guard gives chase; the guard's unconscious body crashes out moments later.

scenes.heading: INT. FALCONE'S DRAWING R OOM - SA ME TIME
scenes.summary: Selina sits with Falcone claiming to be worried about Annika and asking for his help finding her; as she reaches into her clutch, her hand touches the Glock hidden inside.

scenes.heading: INT. FALCONE'S LOFT - TV ROOM - SIMULTANEOUS
scenes.summary: Falcone watches TV in shock as GCl news plays Selina's voicemail recording of him admitting to being a mafia informant and killing Annika.

scenes.heading: INT. THE CLUB ELECTRICAL ROOM - SIMULTANEOUS
scenes.summary: A rotary saw grinds into metal around power lines, sparks flying and illuminating the Bat cowl inside a duffel bag.

scenes.heading: INT. FALCONE'S LOFT - TV ROOM - SIMULTANEOUS
scenes.summary: Selina points her Glock at the back of Falcone's head, calls him 'Dad,' and reveals she's Maria Kyle's daughter; Falcone and his bodyguard turn in shock as she keeps the gun trained on him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Selina reveals her identity as Falcone's daughter in a confrontation that personalizes her vendetta and threatens to end in patricide.

scenes.heading: INT. THE CLUB B ELOW - IMMEDIATELY
scenes.summary: The club goes dark as panicked crowds react; Batman enters the elevator shaft and fires his grappling gun up toward the elevator car five stories above.

scenes.heading: INT. FALCONE'S DARKENED TV ROOM UPSTAIRS - SIMULTANEOUS
scenes.summary: Selina fights Falcone's bodyguard in the darkened room, breaks his knee, knocks him out, then fires blindly at Falcone as he escapes; in the hallway, panicked guards react to gunshots and see Batman emerging from the pitch-dark elevator, then open fire as he charges.

scenes.heading: INT. ICEBERG LOUNGE - LONG ENTRY HALL - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Batman propels Falcone through the club's entry hall past stunned onlookers including Penguin; Falcone taunts Batman saying all his secrets will go to the grave, then smirks at Gordon waiting with a shotgun before both shove him outside where a sniper's bullet kills Falcone instantly.
scenes.turningPointNote: Falcone is assassinated by a sniper the moment he's brought outside, eliminating the key witness and escalating the conspiracy to a new level of danger.

scenes.heading: EXT. PUSHING IN ON THE FRONT OF A LONELY DINER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A lone man sits at a diner counter as armed cops and Batman approach from outside, staring at him through the window.

scenes.heading: INT. LONELY CORNER DINER - THE COUNTER - SIMULTANEOUS
scenes.summary: The lone man at the diner counter meticulously stirs his latte while ignoring screaming cops ordering him to put his hands up, then calmly sips his drink.

scenes.heading: INT. RIDDLER'S STUDIO APARTMENT - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Batman enters Riddler's apartment crawling with investigators, scans notebooks titled 'RENEWAL,' learns the suspect is Edward Nashton who works as a forensic accountant, then discovers a phone livestreaming to hundreds of followers and a message on the screen: 'YOU THINK I'M FINISHED BUT PERHAPS YOU DON'T KNOW THE FULL TRUTH EVERY ENDING IS A NEW BEGINNING SOMETHING IS COMING.'
scenes.turningPointNote: Batman discovers Riddler has been livestreaming and has followers, revealing the threat is far from over despite the arrest.

scenes.heading: INT. VISITING CELL - ARKHAM PRISON FOR THE INSANE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman waits as Riddler enters the visiting cell at Arkham with an eerie smile; Riddler says he's been invisible his whole life but won't be anymore, then mentions Bruce Wayne's name twice while Batman tenses, before revealing he was at Thomas Wayne's mayoral announcement and felt forgotten after the murder, then describes his plan to expose Gotham's lies and unite the city against its corrupt leaders.
scenes.turningPointNote: Riddler reveals his origin tied to Thomas Wayne and explains his plan was to expose corruption and inspire others, not just punish individuals — reframing the entire threat.

scenes.heading: INT. RIDDLER'S DARKENED APARTMENT - FRONT DOOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman breaks into Riddler's sealed apartment searching desperately for what he missed; Martinez interrupts but backs down, then mentions Mitchell was killed with a carpet tool, which strikes Batman who realizes the tool is actually a car jack used to flood the city by destroying the seawall, then discovers online posts from Riddler's followers planning to attack Gotham Square Garden on election night.
scenes.turningPointNote: Batman realizes Riddler's true plan: his followers will attack the election night gathering at the Garden while flooding the city, turning the investigation into a race against time.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTSIDE GOTHAM SQUARE GARDEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chaos erupts outside Gotham Square Garden as emergency vehicles arrive, first responders herd terrified citizens to shelter, and Selina on her motorcycle tries to leave town but is stopped at a roadblock.

scenes.heading: INT. GOTHAM SQUARE GARDEN - HIGH ABOVE - SAME
scenes.summary: Hooded gunmen position themselves in the Garden rafters with rifles and ammunition while Gordon and Bella Real argue with a Fire Marshall about closing the doors as water breaches; Batman arrives and warns them of the attack, then the lights go out, gunfire erupts, and Batman fights the shooters in darkness while Selina arrives and joins the fight, ultimately saving a young boy as explosions destroy the seawall and water floods in.
scenes.turningPointNote: The climactic action sequence where Riddler's plan fully unfolds — armed followers attack the Garden while the seawall explodes, flooding the city and forcing Batman to save lives rather than pursue vengeance.

scenes.heading: EXT. EXTREME HIGH AND WIDE OVER LOWER GOTHAM - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: Dawn breaks over lower Gotham, which is completely submerged in water, as Bruce's voiceover states the national guard is coming.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER GOTHAM STREET S - A SERIES OF SHOTS - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: Flooded key locations from the story appear serene in sunrise light — half-sunk City Hall, a dog paddling past traffic lights, the diner where Riddler was caught, and the empty Iceberg Lounge dance floor — as Bruce's voiceover describes martial law and rampant crime.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM HOSPITAL TRIAGE CENTER - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: Bella Real holds a press conference at a hospital triage center, surrounded by Gordon and embattled officials, promising to rebuild not just the city but people's faith in institutions and each other.

scenes.heading: EXT. ABOVE GOTHAM SQUARE GARDEN - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. ARKHAM PRISON FOR THE INSANE - SAME
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. RUN-DOWN GOTHAM CEMETERY - DUSK
